chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Hi, In this video we will talk about Merge Sort.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: First we have to understand what is Merging?

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: For this I take two lists.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Here are two lists A, which has m elements, i.e. 4 elements.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: B, which has n elements, i.e. 4.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Both lists A and B have sorted elements.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: In Merging, we combine these two lists to make the third list C.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And everytime we merge, we get the third list.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And two lists are merged.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So Merging is a process of combining two sorted lists into a single sorted list.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now let's see what is the procedure.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: In the first list, we will put an integer pointer on the first element of A.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And in the second list, we will put a pointer on the first element of B.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And in the first list, we will put a pointer on the first element of C.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now we will compare A of i and B of j.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: A of i is smaller than B of j, so that element will come here.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And we will move this i forward.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Similarly, k will also move to the next free location.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now we will compare A of i with B of j.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: B of j is smaller than A of i, so that element will be copied here.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And j will move forward.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And k will also move forward.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So we will run this process in the same way.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: For this, I will write a code.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I will write a code for the process that we have just run.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If A of i is less than B of j,

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: then we will copy A of i in C of k.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And we will move k and i forward.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Otherwise, if A is not less than B of j,

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: then we will copy B of j in C of k.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: We will copy all the elements in the same way.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: We will copy 5, 7, 8, 10, etc.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: After copying all these elements,

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: i will remain at 15,

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: and j will remain at 12.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And k will come to the next free location.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now, when these two elements are compared,

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 15 and 12, i.e. A of i with B of j,

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: B of j is smaller,

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: then this element will come in C of 12,

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: and j will move forward.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now, we will see that all the elements are in list B,

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: and j has gone out of the list.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now, we will stop this process.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: We will run this process repeatedly

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: until i is less than m,

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: and j is less than n.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This process will continue until any list is completed.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: As soon as a list is completed,

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: some elements will remain in the next list.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If there was another element 17,

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: then it would have been copied 15 and 17 would have remained.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Right?

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This means that after completing this much process,

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: some elements will remain in the next list.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: At least one element will remain.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now, we have to copy the remaining elements.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Either they will be in A or B.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: For that, I will write the code here.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: While i is less than m,

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: C of k++ assign A of i++.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If elements are in A, then they will be copied in C.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Similarly, if elements are in B list,

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: then they will be copied in C.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is the process of merging.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If we make an algorithm for this,

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: then it becomes a Merge Sort algorithm.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The algorithm will merge A, B, C and results in C.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is the process of merging.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Merging is like this.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let's take some more information about this.

chunks.text: This will give us a new list.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let's give it a name, F.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: we can also merge like this.

chunks.text: Then, we will merge A and B with C.

chunks.text: This will merge A and B with E.

chunks.title: Sort Algorithm - Hindi - Abdul Bari

text: Hi, In this video we will talk about Merge Sort. First we have to understand what is Merging? For this I take two lists. Here are two lists A, which has m elements, i.e. 4 elements. B, which has n elements, i.e. 4. Both lists A and B have sorted elements. In Merging, we combine these two lists to make the third list C. And everytime we merge, we get the third list. And two lists are merged. So Merging is a process of combining two sorted lists into a single sorted list. Now let's see what is the procedure. In the first list, we will put an integer pointer on the first element of A. And in the second list, we will put a pointer on the first element of B. And in the first list, we will put a pointer on the first element of C. Now we will compare A of i and B of j. A of i is smaller than B of j, so that element will come here. And we will move this i forward. Similarly, k will also move to the next free location. Now we will compare A of i with B of j. B of j is smaller than A of i, so that element will be copied here. And j will move forward. And k will also move forward. So we will run this process in the same way. For this, I will write a code. I will write a code for the process that we have just run. If A of i is less than B of j, then we will copy A of i in C of k. And we will move k and i forward. Otherwise, if A is not less than B of j, then we will copy B of j in C of k. We will copy all the elements in the same way. We will copy 5, 7, 8, 10, etc. After copying all these elements, i will remain at 15, and j will remain at 12. And k will come to the next free location. Now, when these two elements are compared, 15 and 12, i.e. A of i with B of j, B of j is smaller, then this element will come in C of 12, and j will move forward. Now, we will see that all the elements are in list B, and j has gone out of the list. Now, we will stop this process. We will run this process repeatedly until i is less than m, and j is less than n. This process will continue until any list is completed. As soon as a list is completed, some elements will remain in the next list. If there was another element 17, then it would have been copied 15 and 17 would have remained. Right? This means that after completing this much process, some elements will remain in the next list. At least one element will remain. Now, we have to copy the remaining elements. Either they will be in A or B. For that, I will write the code here. While i is less than m, C of k++ assign A of i++. If elements are in A, then they will be copied in C. Similarly, if elements are in B list, then they will be copied in C. This is the process of merging. If we make an algorithm for this, then it becomes a Merge Sort algorithm. The algorithm will merge A, B, C and results in C. This is the process of merging. Merging is like this. Let's take some more information about this. Suppose we have 4 lists. A, B, C, D. How will we merge these? First, we will merge A and B. This will give us a new list. Let's give it a name, E. Then, we will merge C and D. This will give us a new list. Let's give it a name, F. Now, we have 2 lists. Earlier, we had 4 lists. When we made E, we got 3 lists. E, C, D. Then, we merged these 2 lists. Now, we have 2 lists. We will merge these 2 lists. This will give us a new list, G. All the elements in G will be combined. This is how we merge. One thing to note here is that we can also merge like this. First, we will merge A and B. Then, we will merge A and B with C. Then, we will merge A and B with F. This will merge A and B with E. E and C will merge with F. F and D will merge with G. This means that if there are more than 2 lists, we can merge them in different ways. I will write here that there can be more than one merge pattern. This point is very important. Let's move ahead. We will elaborate this topic. Next, I... In this example, I have taken 8 lists. Each list has only 1 element. There can be more than 1 element in a list. When there is only 1 element, the list is already sorted. Now, we will see how to merge this. If I merge these 2, then I will get 3 and 8. If I merge these 2, then I will get 5 and 7. Then, these 2 will merge. 2 and 9. 4 and 6. Now, I have 4 lists. I will merge these 2. 3, 5, 8, 7, 8. These 2 will merge. 2, 4, 6, 9. Now, there are 2 lists. These 2 will merge. If we pay attention here, we will find that there are 2 lists. To merge these, we have to write the result in the 3rd list. Here is the single list. We merged these 8 lists. We have a single list. All the elements are sorted. Let's see how many lists have been merged. Here is one merge. 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7. 7 merged. 7 merged for 8 lists. We can merge these in different ways. First, these 2 merge. Then, these 2 merge. Then, the result of these 2 will merge. First, this. Second, this. First, this. Second, this. Third, this. Then, this 4th merge. Then, this 5th merge. Then, this 6th merge. Then, these 2 merge to make 7th merge. Similarly, we can merge these 2. We can merge these 2 to make 7th merge. We can merge like this. Or, First, we merge these 2. The result of this will merge with this. Then, this result will merge with this. I told you earlier that if there are more than 2 lists, then there can be more than 1 pattern to merge them. Now, finally, we move towards merge sort. This is an array. It has a size of 8. It has 8 elements. We have to sort it. I will not see that this is a single array. I will treat it like this. It has 8 different lists. It has 8 different lists. And, each list has an element. Finally, we will see how merge sort works. Here, an array is given. We have to sort it. We will not see that this is a single array with 8 elements. Instead, we will see that each element is a list. Each element is a list. We will treat every element as a list. When we treat every element as a list, then it becomes 8 lists. How will it merge sort? When an array is given, then it will divide the array. It is not a physical division. It will logically divide. It is a phyla division. The left hand side is a list. Then, it will divide the left hand side list again. This is a third division. Here, too, The left hand side and the right hand side are elements. This will change to 3 and 8. Let's see how this process works. Merge sort. If L is 1 and H is 8, then we will give L and H. Then, we will see that there is at least one element in this. L is less than H. If yes, then it will find mid. L plus H by 2. It will divide. After dividing, the left hand side has to be divided again. It has to be divided again. Then, it will apply merge sort again. This is merge sort on low to mid. Right. Similarly, the right hand side will further divide. This is the fourth division. This is the fourth. These two elements are one by one. It will merge them. This will be 5 and 7. This will be 5 and this will be 7. It will call merge sort on the right hand side. Mid plus 1 to high. It will combine the left and right hand side and make a single list. It will merge them. It will merge from low to mid and mid plus 1 to high. This is the merge sort procedure. The left hand side has been merged. It will come to the right hand side and divide from here. It is dividing for the fifth time. It will divide the left side. It is dividing for the sixth time. Then, both of them will merge. The result will be 2 and 9. It will divide the days. It will be the seventh. Then, both of them will merge. It will be 4 and 6. Then, both of them will merge. It will be 2, 4, 6, 9. Then, both of them will merge. It will be a single list and sorted list. This merge sort algorithm will follow this pattern and merge all the elements. It will be a single list and sorted list. The problem here is when these two lists are merging where should we keep the result? For this, we need to take an auxiliary array. We will take an array B where the merge result will be kept. It will be 3, 5, 7, 8. Then, it will be copied back to A. Then, it will go back to A. Similarly, 2, 4, 6, 9 will be copied here. Then, the result will go back. When these two lists merge, the result will come here in B. Then, it will go back to B. To merge, we need an extra auxiliary array. This is how merge sort works. In basic choice, we have to understand merge sort. Merge sort uses a pattern and merges the list and sorts it. When we give a list of 8 elements, it does not consider it as a single list of 8 elements. Instead, it considers it as a list of 8 different lists with single elements each.
